{{Quote|Hey. Watch this.|Rikel's last words before firing Centerpoint|<ref name="Fury" />}}
'''Rikel''', known as '''Vibro''' to many, was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] crewman aboard [[Centerpoint Station]] during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], serving as the head [[Technician/Legends|technician]]. After the [[Death/Legends|loss]] of his [[Rikel's wife|wife]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Rikel was in a position to use the [[Space station/Legends|space station's]] destructive capabilities against the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], which he did when the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] tried to kill one of the joint [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chiefs of State]] of the Galactic Alliance, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], in a [[Battle of the rendezvous point|battle]] with the Galactic Alliance at a point in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] between Coruscant and [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. Rikel was given the chance to fire the station again during the [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Centerpoint Station]], but due to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] by the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Toval Seyah]], this caused the destruction of the station, and Rikel's death.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Think of the station as a body. It has a brain. We're a second brain trying to take over the body, and the first brain is resisting. We take over an area, and the brain retaliates by doing something to foul us up. In this case, we assumed control of the trigger finger… and when we fired, to retaliate, to mess us up, it stuck its thumb in our eye.|Rikel, to Prime Minister Koyan|<ref name="Fury" />}}
Rikel was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual who was secretly [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to a [[Rikel's wife|woman]] because neither his family, nor hers, approved of them being together. Early in the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], which began<ref name="Fury" /> in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]<ref name="Date" /> between the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] and the [[Confederation]]—a regime that formed to aid the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and its [[Five Worlds]] government in its rebellion against the Galactic Alliance—Rikel's wife was arrested during a security sweep on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Galactic Alliance. The [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] had begun raiding Corellian neighborhoods on Coruscant after the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] bombed [[The Elite (hotel)|The Elite]], a [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] in [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]]. Rikel's wife was later found [[Death/Legends|dead]], which instilled in him a hatred for [[Coruscanti/Legends|Coruscanti]], and for the Galactic Alliance.<ref name="Fury" />

[[File:CenterpointStation-SoF.png|thumb|left|Centerpoint Station]]
Later in the war, Rikel was able to obtain a position on [[Centerpoint Station]] as the head [[Technician/Legends|technician]] in the [[fire-control chamber]] under the nickname "Vibro," which he liked more than his own name. Centerpoint Station was an ancient [[Space station/Legends|space station]] that was capable of moving entire planets with its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]], as well as creating [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] fluctuations which could tear apart [[Star/Legends|stars]], planets, and [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]]. The station fired on the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] and the [[Second Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Galactic Alliance Second Fleet]] in a [[Battle of the rendezvous point|battle]] at a point in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] between Coruscant and Corellia. The ''Anakin Solo'' was the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of one of the joint [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chiefs of State]] of the Galactic Alliance, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], who was Centerpoint's intended target. However, the station overloaded, a side effect of trying to override the security locks which had imprinted on the young [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Solo]] during the [[Corellian Crisis|First Corellian Insurrection]], when he used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to take control of the station. However, Centerpoint Station did not obliterate the ''Anakin Solo'', Jacen Solo was not killed, and the Galactic Alliance found out that the station was operational again. After [[Sadras Koyan]], [[Five World Prime Minister|Prime Minister of the Five Worlds]], asked what had happened to the station, Rikel gave him a more simplistic explanation of the events: that of a body trying to be controlled by two brains.<ref name="Fury" />

With the knowledge that Centerpoint Station was operational once more, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] Solo formed a plan for retaliation—the Galactic Alliance would [[Battle of Centerpoint Station (Second Galactic Civil War)|capture]] the station, and use it to force Confederation worlds to surrender, thus ending the war. Due to the station's destructive capabilities, the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi]] could not allow Solo to gain control of Centerpoint, especially after he held the students of the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] [[Battle of Ossus (Second Galactic Civil War)|hostage]]. They also could not permit anyone else to control the station, and planned to destroy it at the same [[Time/Legends|time]] that Solo was attempting to capture it. To this end, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyp Durron]] escorted the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Toval Seyah]], who had extensive knowledge of the station and its systems, around Centerpoint.<ref name="Fury" />

As the battle drew on, [[Prime Minister/Legends|Prime Minister]] Koyan tried contacting Solo's fellow co–Chief of State, [[Cha Niathal]], in an attempt to force Niathal to surrender. When she didn't answer, Rikel, fueled by his desire to avenge his wife's death, advocated that they blow up Coruscant anyway, and suggested that since the Confederation had reconnaissance [[Starship/Legends|ships]] in the area, they would be able to get recordings of it. Koyan was aghast at such a statement, but received an incoming [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] transmission from [[Denjax Teppler]], the Five Worlds' [[Minister of Information (Five Worlds)|Minister of Information]], warning him to leave the station before the Galactic Alliance could bring him to a war trial. After their private discussion, Koyan instructed Rikel to take orders from Teppler and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Genna Delpin|Delpin]], the [[Supreme Commander of the Corellian Defense Force]], until hearing from him again, and said that they would probably give him the chance to fire on Coruscant.<ref name="Fury" />

Rikel was not privy to the conversation between Koyan and Teppler, but he followed Koyan on the station's security [[Holocamera/Legends|cams]] until Koyan reached [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] [[Epsilon 34G]] and boarded an [[Chinnith|Alliance shuttle]], believing it was his escape vessel. No one had heard what Koyan had told him, so Rikel took the opportunity to fire Centerpoint Station. Once the power source was ready, he got the attention of his [[Unidentified female technician|fellow technician]], and pressed the button. Yet Seyah had been able to reprogram the star data in Centerpoint's systems, and reset all the other coordinates as the center of [[Hollowtown]], an open sphere in the exact center of the station itself. Hence, the station targeted itself when fired, causing it to destroy itself from within and killing everyone onboard.<ref name="Fury" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Destroy Coruscant. Show them what this station is made of. We have reconnaissance ships in the Coruscant system, don't we? They'll get excellent recordings.|Rikel, to Koyan, during the Battle of Centerpoint Station|<ref name="Fury" />}}
Rikel was a male with [[Color/Legends|yellow]] skin, long [[Hair/Legends|hair]] tied into a braid, and a beard. After marrying his wife in secret, she was arrested on Coruscant and later found dead. This drove him to hate all Coruscanti, and the Galactic Alliance to which they belonged. He went as far as attempting to destroy their capital planet with Centerpoint Station in revenge, making all off-world Coruscanti planetless, but doing so caused his own death. Rikel was also fond of [[Caf/Legends|caf]], and after his eighth cup of caf, he would embody his own nickname, "Vibro."<ref name="Fury" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rikel was created by [[Aaron Allston]] for the novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'', which was released on [[November 27]], [[2007]] as the seventh entry in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series.<ref name="Fury" />
